**IF YOU DO NOT ASK THE ANSWER IS ALWAYS NO!**‚Äã

‚Äã

**BOOK I AM READING THIS WEEK**

Finished reading [India's Most Fearless 2: More Military Stories of Unimaginable Courage and Sacrifice](https://amzn.to/3DDhD3f)**‚Äã  
‚Äã**Good read, if you like patriotic stories of valor and incredible courage!

Started reading **[Cold Steel](https://amzn.to/3SGT89z)** (thanks to Aditya Kondawar for the suggestion) - which is the takeover story of Mittal Steel (Lakshmi Mittal) taking over Arcelor.   
It is written in a gripping fashion and I haven't been able to put it down! Brilliant book with a lot of research gone behind it.   
Pick it up if you love true stories about corporate takeovers :)

In other news, I was at the press for 2 days, signing the copies of my next book 'Get Epic Shit Done', which will launch for pre-order soon with 25K signed copies up for grabs (and will start shipping Dec 17th).

[![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!4qIl!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F562204b5-0aa2-483f-98ec-b01b6fa1fbf2_567x793.jpeg)](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!4qIl!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F562204b5-0aa2-483f-98ec-b01b6fa1fbf2_567x793.jpeg)

‚Äã

**Do Epic Shit is FINALLY in audio (yay!).   
‚Äã**I had an awesome time recording it in my voice and it is exclusively on Audible.   
If you are into audio books - consider listening to this one :)**‚Äã  
‚Äã[adbl.co/doepicshit](https://example.com/blog/2022-09-29-can-someone-pay-my-bill/adbl.co/doepicshit)‚Äã**

**If you prefer printed books then pick up the book in [English](https://amzn.to/3O3nyzU)** (Hardcover, Paperback, Kindle) **[Hindi](https://amzn.to/3yCg5lW), [Marathi](https://amzn.to/3OYeaPs) and [Tamil](https://amzn.to/3yYTYat).   
200,000+ copies sold.  
5 more languages coming up! (Including Portuguese)**

*‚Äã*

**RESPONSES TO LAST WEEK'S QUESTION**

If you are sad, what do you prefer?

1. To be left alone
2. Have someone to cheer me up/to talk to

Here are the responses:

[![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!RpWI!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F8acd0802-09f2-4d81-8c97-59bdd2e8d25e_956x122.png)](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!RpWI!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F8acd0802-09f2-4d81-8c97-59bdd2e8d25e_956x122.png)

‚Äã

Observations:

* Fascinating! As we grow older, we prefer having people around us to cheer us up!
* While the younger folks are more like - nope I am good. Will handle this on my own.
* I think the reason is two-fold:
  
  + While young, we actually think people are going to judge us if we are sad and desire company to make us feel good
  + When old, we have built some meaningful relationships (hopefully) that we can bank upon
* That said, there is still a pretty strong percentage of people who prefer to be left alone. Which is perfectly fine, UNLESS - you are not sharing because you believe you will be a burden on others.
* Trust me - you will be surprised at how many people are willing to help, if you ask for it!

My response?  
I prefer being left alone and processing it on my own - because I am able to quickly resolve it and move on. That is a superpower :)

‚Äã

**PICTURE OF THE WEEK**

![](/img/missing-image.png)

s Sunday we went to the Bangla Sahib Gurudwara in Delhi. It is a beautiful serene places (as most Gurudwaras tend to be) - the langar was awesome (especially the dal) and the weather was just perfect for the setting!

Have you visited the Golden Temple in Amritsar? It is a must-visit.

**QUESTION OF THE WEEK**

Do you know what's special about you?

1. Yes
2. Not yet
3. Nothing special about me
